subject: early-literacy
script: This is the letter B. B makes the buh sound like ball.

subject: math
script: Let us count slowly together. One, two, three.

subject: math
script: This is a circle. It is round like a wheel or a ball.
media_hint: big round moon and ball animation

subject: phonics
script: Cat has the short a sound. Say it with me: c-a-t.

subject: math
script: You had six and then got four more. Six plus four makes ten.

subject: reading
script: Goal is a simple word you can read. Say each sound: g-o-a-l.
media_hint: soccer goal sign with bouncing letters

subject: reading
script: The main idea is what the whole paragraph is mostly about.

subject: math
script: Three groups of four means four plus four plus four. That is twelve.

subject: logic
script: Look for what repeats and choose what comes next in the pattern.
media_hint: space tiles blinking in a repeat pattern

subject: math
script: When the pieces are the same size, the fraction with more pieces shaded is greater.
media_hint: builder tiles split into equal parts

subject: reading
script: Look at the sentence before and after the word to figure out what it most likely means.

subject: world-knowledge
script: Engineers test designs to make sure they are safe, strong, and work the way they should.

subject: early-literacy
script: This is the letter A. A makes the ah sound like apple.
media_hint: big red apple with letter A animation

subject: math
script: This is a triangle. It has three sides and three corners.
media_hint: colorful triangle tent with three corners highlighted

subject: math
script: Let us count slowly together. One, two, three, four, five.
media_hint: five jumping frogs counted one at a time

subject: phonics
script: Bed has the short e sound. Say it with me: b-e-d.
media_hint: sports scoreboard with bed mascot card

subject: math
script: Ten minus four means taking four away from ten. Six are left.

subject: reading
script: Look at each letter. Say the sound. Put them together to read the word.
media_hint: sports card with letter tiles building a short word

subject: reading
script: A cause is what made something happen. An effect is what happened because of it.
media_hint: rocket launch timeline with cause and effect arrows

subject: math
script: Line up the ones, tens, and hundreds. Add each column from right to left.

subject: math
script: The tenths place is the first digit after the decimal point. The hundredths place is the second.
media_hint: building blueprint with decimal grid overlay

subject: reading
script: Text evidence is a detail from the passage that proves your answer. Look for exact words.
media_hint: construction blueprint with highlighted evidence lines

subject: math
script: Count each picture one time. Point as you count: one, two, three.

subject: math
script: Count all five items slowly. Keep your finger moving one by one.

subject: math
script: A circle is round and has no corners. It looks like a wheel or a ball.

subject: math
script: A triangle has three sides and three corners. Look for the pointed shape.

subject: phonics
script: Short a sounds like apple and cat. Listen for the open ah sound.

subject: math
script: Find the two parts that make ten. You can count up from the bigger number.

subject: math
script: Ten minus something means taking that many away from ten. Count what is left.

subject: phonics
script: Short e sounds like bed and hen. Listen for the eh sound in the middle.

subject: reading
script: The main idea is what the whole passage is mostly about, not one tiny detail.

subject: math
script: Three groups of four mean four plus four plus four. That makes twelve.

subject: math
script: Line up the hundreds, tens, and ones. Add carefully and regroup when needed.

subject: reading
script: A cause makes something happen. Ask what happened first and what changed next.

subject: math
script: When pieces are the same size, the fraction with more shaded pieces is greater.

subject: math
script: The digit after the decimal starts in the tenths place. The second digit is the hundredths place.

subject: reading
script: Good evidence is a detail from the passage that directly proves the answer.

subject: reading
script: Use the nearby words in the sentence to narrow the meaning of the tricky word.

subject: math
script: Count each item one by one and stop when you say three.

subject: math
script: Touch each picture once and keep counting until five.

subject: early-literacy
script: A makes the short a sound in words like apple and ant.

subject: early-literacy
misconception: mixing up B with similar letters
script: B makes the buh sound in words like ball and book.

subject: math
script: A circle is round with no corners, like a wheel or a coin.

subject: math
script: A triangle has three sides and three corners, like a roof or a tent.

subject: math
misconception: not seeing pairs that make ten
script: Make ten by finding pairs that fit together to make 10.

subject: math
script: Start with 10 and count back to find what is left.

subject: phonics
misconception: mixing short a with other vowel sounds
script: Listen for the short a sound in words like cat, map, and jam.

subject: phonics
misconception: mixing short e with other vowel sounds
script: Short e sounds like bed, hen, and pen.

subject: reading
script: Look at each letter and blend the sounds left to right.

subject: reading
script: Read the word as a whole and check the letters carefully.

subject: math
script: Line up hundreds, tens, and ones before you add.

subject: reading
misconception: mixing up what happened first and next
script: Look for what happened and what came next because of it.

subject: reading
misconception: focusing on tiny details instead of the big idea
script: Read for the big idea. Ask what the passage is mostly about.

subject: math
script: Break the groups apart and count equal sets to find the product.

subject: logic
script: Spot what repeats and choose the next item in the pattern.

subject: math
misconception: reading the fraction parts without comparing size
script: Bigger fractions have more equal parts or bigger parts when the top stays the same.

subject: math
script: Look at the place value name first, then read the digit in that spot.

subject: world-knowledge
misconception: thinking models are only for looks
script: Engineers test designs, models, and materials to make things safe and strong.

subject: reading
misconception: choosing a sentence that sounds nice but does not prove the claim
script: Choose the sentence that directly proves the claim in the passage.

subject: reading
misconception: guessing without reading the sentence around the word
script: Use the sentence around a word to figure out its meaning.

subject: math
script: Bigger means more. Smaller means less. Count each group to find out which is more or less.
media_hint: two animal groups of different sizes side by side

subject: early-literacy
script: Rhyming words end with the same sound. Cat and hat both end in at. Listen for the ending sound.
media_hint: bouncing word pairs with highlighted endings

subject: world-knowledge
script: Each color has a name. Red is the color of apples. Blue is the color of the sky. Say the color name out loud.
media_hint: colorful objects paired with their color names

subject: math
script: A number bond shows two parts that make a whole. Three and two make five. Four and one also make five.
media_hint: number bond diagram with connecting circles

subject: phonics
script: Short i sounds like ih. Sit has the short i sound. Say it with me: s-i-t.
media_hint: word sit with glowing middle letter i

subject: reading
script: Sight words are common words we see all the time. You learn them by looking and remembering, not sounding out.
media_hint: flashcard flip animation with common sight words

subject: math
script: The short hand shows the hour. The long hand shows the minutes. When the long hand points to 12, it is exactly on the hour.
media_hint: analog clock with animated hands pointing to hours

subject: math
script: Skip counting means jumping by the same number each time. Count by fives: 5, 10, 15, 20. Each jump adds five.
media_hint: number line with hops of five highlighted

subject: math
script: Greater than means more. Less than means fewer. Use the symbols: the open side faces the bigger number.
media_hint: number line with two numbers and comparison arrow

subject: math
script: Percent means out of one hundred. To find a percent of a number, multiply the number by the percent and divide by 100.
media_hint: hundred-square grid with shaded percent region

subject: reading
script: An inference uses clues from the text plus what you already know to figure out something the author did not say directly.
media_hint: text passage with detective magnifying glass on key clues

subject: math
script: A ratio compares two quantities. Two red marbles to three blue means for every two red ones there are three blue ones.
media_hint: colored marble groups showing the ratio side by side

subject: math
script: Multiply by making equal groups and counting all the items.

subject: math
misconception: not noticing the repeating part of the pattern
script: Find the repeat and choose the next item that fits the pattern.

subject: math
misconception: comparing the bottom number without thinking about part size
script: Compare the size of the parts to decide which fraction is larger.

subject: reading
misconception: ignoring the sentence around the word
script: Use the nearby sentence clues to infer the meaning of a word.

subject: world-knowledge
misconception: thinking a prototype is only for looks
script: Engineers build and test models so they can improve the design before the final build.
